findings.evidence: Lines 65-85 define `@[reducible] def instGroupLike` and already provide `@[simp] instGroupLike_coact` plus `@[simp] groupLikeCoact_apply` as the public characterization.
findings.fix: Remove `@[reducible]`; keep/use `instGroupLike_coact` and `groupLikeCoact_apply`, adding any direct apply lemma needed instead of exposing the instance body.
findings.issue: `instGroupLike` is marked `@[reducible]`, so consumers can depend on the implementation of the comodule instance rather than the characteristic coaction lemmas.

summary: The characteristic lemmas are mostly present, but the group-like comodule structure exposes its body through reducibility instead of making users rely on the public coaction API.
